content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

October 24, 2002

Barbara Riegel Wayne, CMC
City of Oceanside
Department of the City Clerk
300 North Coast Highway
Oceanside, CA 92054-2885

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-02-280

Dear Ms. Wayne:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).¹

Specifically, you are requesting advice as to whether another person - the Oceanside Police Officer’s Association Political Action Committee (“PAC”) - is required to report on its campaign statement the cost of a certain advertisement as an expenditure. The question presented is complex or is otherwise inappropriate for resolution by informal assistance. Therefore, we decline to provide you with the informal assistance you request. (Regulations 18329(b)(8) and (c)(4)(E), respectively - copies enclosed.)

To discharge your duty pursuant to section 81010(c), you should notify the committee that the campaign statement may be incorrect or incomplete, and inform the committee that it may seek written advice from the Commission regarding the Act’s reporting requirements. (Regulation 18110(c).) If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Kenneth L. Glick
Counsel, Legal Division

KLG:jg
I:\AdviceLtrs\02-280

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
